replies: Hey, you can call me Maya. I just moved to Rotterdam about three weeks ago — doing a master's in urban design at TU Delft, so I'm commuting but living here.
I'm in Oude Westen, near Witte de Withstraat. The cafés on that street basically convinced me to take the flat.
I love long walks with my camera, mostly film stuff. I'll spend a whole Sunday wandering the Maashaven docks just looking for light. Slow coffee places are my other weakness.
I also read a lot — mostly architecture and city planning books, which fits the degree. And I'm slowly trying to figure out Dutch.
Weeknight evenings are good after class — say from seven onwards — and most of Sunday. English mostly, decent Spanish, learning Dutch.
Hi, I'm Daan. I've been in Rotterdam my whole life — 34 now, work as a copywriter for a small agency in Kralingen.
I live in Crooswijk, in one of those side streets near the park. Honestly the quietest bit of the city if you know where to look.
Big into photography — Leica nerd, sorry — and just walking the city. I've done a loose project of the same five bridges over three years. Coffee, books, the slow stuff.
I'd like to meet a few new people, not for any big reason, just feel like my circle's gone quiet. Happy to show newcomers around too.
Weeknight evenings and weekend afternoons both work. Dutch and English, conversational German.
I'm Priya. Moved over from Bangalore for a job at a Rotterdam firm — UX design at a fintech. About four months in.
Living in Kop van Zuid, twentieth floor, view of the Erasmusbrug if you lean. It's slightly mad but I love it.
I take a lot of phone photos, mostly architectural details — I find the brick patterns here completely different from what I grew up with. Long walks along the water. Slow weekend coffees.
I've started reading more — mostly Dutch authors in translation, trying to figure the country out. And I'd love to find people who do photo walks together.
Weekday evenings are stretched but Saturday and Sunday daytimes are wide open. English fluent, Hindi, picking up basic Dutch.
I'm Tomas, just call me Tom. Came from Prague for a PhD in architecture, started in February.
Renting a small studio in Cool district, right next to the Lijnbaan. Walking distance to almost everything.
I'm into photography — Czech tradition has a thing about it — and I read constantly, mostly philosophy of urban space. Long walks help me think. Coffee, mostly black.
I'd genuinely like a small group to wander the city with. I don't need talkative, just companion-y.
Weekday evenings after seven, both weekend days. Czech, English, intermediate German, no Dutch yet.
Sofia here. I moved from Bologna last summer with my partner who got a job here. I'm doing freelance graphic design.
We're in Delfshaven, in one of the old houses by the canal. The whole area feels quietly alive.
Photography is the through-line — I shoot medium format. I read a lot, mainly Italian fiction. And I love a long, slow walk with a coffee stop halfway.
Honestly I want to meet people through doing things rather than going for a drink. A photo walk would be perfect.
I work from home so I'm flexible — weekday afternoons can work, definitely Sunday. Italian, English, learning Dutch.
I'm Lukas. Moved from Poznań to Rotterdam two months ago for a logistics coordinator role at the port. Twenty-eight, first time living abroad.
Living in Beverwaard, the south side. Cheaper, calmer, decent tram in.
I cook a lot — Polish things mostly, but I'm getting into Dutch produce. And I'm a board games guy. Catan, Wingspan, the heavier ones too.
I bake on Sundays — rye bread, pierogi when I'm homesick. I like wine but only the white stuff.
Weeknight evenings free, both weekend days. Polish, English, very basic Dutch.
Hi, Anneke. I'm 41, live in Rotterdam, work in HR. Lived here forever, originally from Friesland.
I'm in Hillegersberg, the leafy bit north of the centre. Two kids, both in primary.
I'd love to meet a few people through cooking and board games — that's genuinely how I unwind. Vegan-ish, I cook a lot of it. Big into baking, especially sourdough.
I'm also one of those Dutch people who actually wants to help newcomers feel a bit at home — friend told me about this app.
Weeknight evenings after the kids are down — eight onwards — and Saturday afternoons. Dutch, English, German.
I'm Joon. Came from Seoul about a year ago for my partner's job at Erasmus MC.
We're in Blijdorp, walking distance to the zoo and Park 1943. I'm not working right now, taking time to settle in.
Cooking is basically my whole identity at the moment — Korean home stuff, but I've started doing fusion with whatever's at the Saturday market. Board games are a weekly ritual with my partner.
I'd love to find people who cook together. And I'm pretty good at wine pairings, even if I shouldn't say so.
Honestly any time, I'm very flexible — weekday daytimes too. Korean, English, learning Dutch.
Mark, 38. From Liverpool originally, in Rotterdam for ten years now. Civil engineer.
I'm in Kralingen, near the lake. Walk around it nearly every morning.
Big home cook — Italian and Middle Eastern lately. I host a monthly board game evening, mostly heavier strategy stuff. Bread baking on the weekends.
I'd like to help newcomers a bit — I remember how lost I was. Wine I'll drink, podcasts I'll talk about endlessly.
Weeknight evenings always free, Saturdays for the bigger gatherings. English, Dutch, basic Italian.
I'm Hana. 26, moved from Brno for a junior data scientist role at a Rotterdam fintech. Started two months ago.
Renting a room near Eendrachtsplein, very central, slightly noisy but I love being able to walk everywhere.
I cook constantly — vegan, partly for health, partly because it's the kind of cooking I'm interested in. Sourdough obsession. Board games every chance I get.
Podcasts on my commute, mainly food and travel ones. White wine on a slow evening.
Most weekday evenings, both weekend days. Czech, English, slowly learning Dutch.
Hi, I'm Sven. Just moved from Hamburg for a music journalism job here — started in March.
I'm in De Pijp, near the Albert Cuyp. Loud, fun, my flat smells like the food market every day, no complaints.
Live music is my actual job and my actual hobby. Jazz, post-rock, anything good in a small room. I cycle everywhere. Drink the local beer like it's water.
I'd love to find a few people who hit shows together — I keep going alone and it's getting old.
Most weeknights after eight, weekends pretty open. German, English, learning Dutch.
Femke. Born in Amsterdam, 33. Work as an illustrator, mostly children's books.
I live in the Jordaan, one of those tiny canal-side flats that always smell like the bakery downstairs.
Live music is the thing — I go to jazz shows almost weekly. Cycling around the city for hours. I like neighbourhood bars over big places. Some art-school habits I haven't shaken — sketching in cafés.
I want to meet new people, my circle has gotten smaller since friends moved out of the city.
Weeknight evenings, Saturday late afternoons. Dutch, English, conversational French.
I'm Adrien. Moved from Lyon two months ago — relocated for a product management role at a fintech in Amsterdam.
I'm in Oud-West, near Bilderdijkstraat. Quiet street, loud weekends.
I'm obsessed with live music — anything from jazz to electronic, the small venues are why I picked Amsterdam over Berlin. Cycling I'm learning to enjoy. Art galleries on slow Sundays.
I like dive bars over fancy ones. And I'd genuinely love to find a few people to share shows with.
Weekday evenings, Saturday all day, Sunday afternoons. French, English, almost no Dutch yet.
I'm Owen. Came from Dublin for a partner who got a job at the Concertgebouw, so we landed in Amsterdam four months ago.
We're in Oud-Zuid, walking distance to the museums. Honestly almost too pretty.
Live music — partner's a violinist so it's the household soundtrack. I lean jazz and rock though. I cycle. Beer over wine. I draw a bit, never seriously.
I'd love to find some people for gigs and bar evenings. Not big into clubs.
Weeknight evenings work, weekends a bit hit-or-miss. English, basic Dutch.
Karin, 52. Amsterdam-born, recently semi-retired from a long career as a museum curator.
I live in De Baarsjes, one of those west-side streets with the old trams.
I want to spend this next stretch of life properly — going to shows, helping new arrivals find their way in. Jazz especially. Long bike rides. I still go to bars I like.
I'm not looking to be anyone's mentor, just to share a city I love.
Most weeknight evenings, weekend afternoons. Dutch, English, German, French.
Hey, I'm Elena. Moved from Madrid four months ago, work in marketing at a SaaS company near Sloterdijk.
Living in Westerpark, three minutes from the park. Run there every morning.
Big runner — half marathons. Yoga twice a week. Started padel since I got here, completely hooked. Hiking on long weekends, the Dunes are amazing.
I'd like to find a few people who do active stuff together. I'm tired of running alone.
Weekday mornings before work, weekend mornings always. Spanish, English, learning Dutch slowly.
I'm Jasper. 29, born and raised Amsterdammer. Software engineer, work fully remote.
I live in Noord, took the ferry-life thing seriously about three years ago. Love it.
I run a lot, swim in the IJ in the summer, started padel about a year ago. Hiking trips when I can. Yoga in the off-mornings.
I just want to meet a few new active people — friend group has thinned a bit since some moved to Berlin.
Weekday mornings (work flexes), weekend mornings, some weekend afternoons. Dutch, English.
Hi, I'm Aida. Came from Sofia two months ago for a partner who's doing a PhD at UvA.
We're in Oost, near Oosterpark. The park is half the reason we chose the flat.
I'm a runner — used to compete on the track. Yoga most days. I picked up padel almost the day I arrived. Long hikes when we can get out of the city.
Looking to meet a few people through sport — I find it the easiest way.
Weekday mornings, both weekend days for runs and games. Bulgarian, English, beginner Dutch.
I'm Lucas. Moved from São Paulo a year ago for a job in renewable energy — engineer.
I live in Oud-West, easy cycle into the centre and out to the parks both ways.
Running is the constant — I do most of the longer ones in Amsterdamse Bos. Padel weekly. Yoga to stay sane. Big into swimming open water.
I'd love to find a small group for active things. The work crowd's nice but mostly indoor people.
Weekday mornings, Saturday all day, Sunday morning. Portuguese, Spanish, English, basic Dutch.
I'm Mei. Moved from Taipei eight months ago for a master's at UvA in psychology.
I'm in De Pijp, sharing with a friend from the programme. Loud, but a block from Sarphatipark.
I run every morning, slowly — Sarphatipark loop. Yoga at a tiny studio on Cornelis Troostplein. I started padel with classmates, surprisingly fun. Love a long hike.
I want to meet people outside the master's bubble.
Weekday mornings before class, weekend mornings, sometimes Sunday afternoons. Mandarin, English, beginner Dutch.
I'm Astrid. Just moved from Stockholm for a policy role at one of the international tribunals here. Three weeks in.
Living in Statenkwartier, near the embassies. Walkable, quiet, slightly stately.
I play chess, badly but enthusiastically. I read constantly, mostly history and contemporary fiction. Museums — Mauritshuis was my first weekend out. I'd love to swap languages with someone.
I'm trying to learn Dutch properly, not just survival phrases. Theatre I miss already.
Weeknight evenings free, Sunday afternoons reliable. Swedish, English, German, learning Dutch.
Wouter, 47. Born in Leiden, lived in The Hague for twenty years. I teach Dutch as a second language at an adult college.
I'm in Bezuidenhout, the older bit near the train station.
Chess is a habit, I play at a small club on Tuesdays. Books endlessly, mostly translated literature. Theatre nights at the Nationale. And language exchange — I do it for fun, not work.
I'd genuinely like to help newcomers find their feet — I see it from the teaching side every day.
Weeknight evenings except Tuesdays, weekend afternoons. Dutch, English, French, German.
I'm Yuki. Moved from Kyoto last year for a job at a research institute — economics. Twenty-nine.
I'm in Archipelbuurt, an apartment with a tiny balcony and too many books.
I play chess online and at the club near Westeinde. Read a lot, museums obsessively — the Mauritshuis is half my weekends. Trying to learn Dutch properly, exchange partner welcome.
I think I want a small group, not a big one. Calm people.
Weeknight evenings, weekend afternoons. Japanese, English, learning Dutch.
Hi, I'm Rafael. Moved from Buenos Aires four months ago for a master's in international law at Leiden, commuting from The Hague.
I live in Centrum, near the Passage. Walkable and full of small bookshops.
Chess is a long-time thing — used to play tournaments. Reading takes up most of my evenings. Museums, theatre when I can. I'd love to swap Spanish for Dutch with someone patient.
I'm not after a big social scene, just a few thoughtful people.
Weeknight evenings after eight, Sunday afternoons. Spanish, English, beginner Dutch.
I'm Sanne. Born here, 61, just retired from a translator's career.
I'm in Benoordenhout, lived in the same flat for thirty years.
Chess at the club, reading more than ever, theatre season tickets. I help with language exchange at the library — Dutch for English mostly. Mauritshuis volunteer one morning a week.
I'd like to meet a few new arrivals through this — I miss the curiosity of newcomers, honestly.
Weekday daytimes mostly free, weekend afternoons. Dutch, English, French, German, decent Italian.
I'm Camila. Moved from Bogotá six months ago for a partner who works at a think-tank here. I'm freelancing as a translator.
We're in Scheveningen, two minutes from the boulevard. Walking on the beach has become a daily ritual.
Tango — I've been dancing for ten years, salsa before that. Latin music in general. I'd love to swap Spanish for Dutch.
Looking for people who dance, or who want to start. The Hague has a small but real scene.
Weeknight evenings, Sunday afternoons. Spanish, English, beginner Dutch.
I'm Tim. 36, from The Hague, software developer.
I'm in Bezuidenhout, lived here most of my life.
I dance salsa, mostly Friday nights. Started tango last year, slowly getting better. Latin music plays in my car constantly. I also do language exchange — picked up Spanish that way.
I want to meet a few new people through dance — that's how I've made every friend I have.
Weeknight evenings, Friday and Saturday nights. Dutch, English, conversational Spanish.
I'm Pia. Came from Naples a year ago, work as a graphic designer at a small studio.
I'm in Regentessekwartier, next to the canal. I cycle to work in ten minutes.
Tango is the thing — I dance four nights a week if I can. Salsa as a warm-up. Latin music, the whole spectrum. Language exchange to keep my Dutch alive — and pick up Portuguese.
I'd love to find a small group who dance and just hang out.
Weeknight evenings, all of Saturday and Sunday. Italian, English, intermediate Dutch.
Hi, I'm Kofi. Moved from Accra two months ago for a doctorate at TU Delft, but I prefer living in The Hague — closer to the beach.
Right by Hollands Spoor in Statenkwartier-ish area, top floor of an old building.
Dance is my non-academic life — salsa mostly, getting into tango, some bachata. Latin music. I'm doing a Dutch class for language exchange too.
I want a small dance-y crowd. The Hague has the right energy for it.
Weeknight evenings, full weekends. English, Twi, beginner Dutch.
I'm Marta. Moved from Lisbon three months ago — my partner works at the OPCW.
We're in Archipelbuurt, lots of leafy streets, very quiet at night.
Tango and salsa for years — danced semi-professionally in my twenties, just for fun now. Latin music always playing. Language exchange — I'd love to maintain my Spanish and start Dutch.
Small group, dance-focused, would be perfect.
Weeknight evenings, Saturday and Sunday afternoons. Portuguese, Spanish, English, basic Dutch.
I'm Ola. Moved from Kraków a month ago — I'm a mid-career architect, just joined a Rotterdam firm doing public-space work.
I'm in the Wijnhaven bit of Centrum, in one of those new towers, view straight at the Erasmusbrug.
Photography is the through-line — mostly architectural, sometimes street. I take long walks with the camera every weekend, all over the city.
I read a lot, mostly history and design. Slow coffee at small places is my reward for being outside in the cold.
Weeknight evenings and Saturday daytime work best. Polish, English, basic German, no Dutch yet.
Hey, I'm Ravi. Came from Bangalore in March for a data-engineering role at a Rotterdam logistics company.
I live in Kralingen, near the lake, about a 20-minute cycle to the office.
I started taking film photos about a year ago — I'm slow at it, which I think is the point. Long walks around the lake are how I think.
I also love film, the proper sit-in-a-cinema kind, and a slow coffee on a Sunday before everyone else is awake.
Weeknight evenings after seven and full Sundays. English, Hindi, Kannada, picking up Dutch words.
I'm Hannah. Born here, 38, work as an editor for an architecture magazine.
I live in Noord, in one of the smaller streets near the Schiekade. Lots of light, quiet evenings.
Photography is my long thing — I shoot medium format, slowly. I do ceramics on the side, terrible but enjoyable.
Reading is a daily habit. Long walks the city are where I clear my head, often with a coffee stop.
Weeknight evenings and Saturday afternoons. Dutch, English, conversational French.
Hi, I'm Mateo. Moved from Mexico City last September to do a master's in film at the Willem de Kooning Academy.
I'm in Cool, very central, paying too much for a tiny flat but I can walk everywhere.
Film is the obvious one — I shoot 16mm when I can afford to. Photography on the side, also analogue. Long walks looking for locations.
Slow coffee places are where I do most of my reading. Art galleries on Sundays.
Weeknight evenings, weekend daytimes, sometimes Sunday evening. Spanish, English, learning Dutch.
I'm Esther. Came from Kyiv five months ago — my partner got work here. I'm freelancing as a photographer.
We're in Delfshaven, in one of the old houses on the canal. The light is incredible in the late afternoon.
Photography is my work and my hobby — I can't really separate them. Film, mostly portraits. Long walks looking for things to shoot.
I read constantly, mainly Ukrainian fiction in translation, and I love a long slow coffee somewhere quiet.
Weekday daytimes work for me, plus most weekends. Ukrainian, Russian, English, basic Dutch.
I'm Bram. Lived in Rotterdam most of my life, recently retired from teaching architectural history at Erasmus.
I'm in Hillegersberg, near the lakes. Walk around them every morning, rain or sun.
I want to help a few newcomers find their footing — I see the city change every year and miss meeting new people.
Photography is a lifelong habit, mostly black-and-white. Books I'm never not reading. Long walks and slow coffee close out the week.
Weekday daytimes, weekday evenings, Saturday afternoons. Dutch, English, French, conversational German.
Yara here. Moved from Beirut six months ago for a junior graphic-design role at a small Rotterdam studio.
I'm in Centrum, just behind the Markthal. Walk to work in eight minutes.
Photography is my way of paying attention to the city — phone mostly, sometimes a small Ricoh. Long walks down to the river.
I sketch in cafés, drink slow coffee, read whatever a friend recommends.
Weeknight evenings, both weekend days. Arabic, French, English, learning Dutch.
I'm Stefan. Came from Vienna a year ago for a city-planner role at the municipality.
I live in Kop van Zuid, fifteenth floor, view of the bridge. Yes, I take photos of it constantly.
Long walks are how I learn a city — I've done most of Rotterdam on foot now. Photography, mostly architectural, fits naturally.
I read a lot of urbanism and urban-history books, often at a slow coffee place near Hofbogen.
Weeknight evenings, weekend daytimes. German, English, basic Dutch.
I'm Carlos. Came from Barcelona four months ago to work as a sous chef at a place in Witte de Withstraat.
I live nearby in Centrum, two minutes from the restaurant which is a blessing and a curse.
Cooking is obviously the job — but my hobby is cooking for friends, slowly, with wine. I bake on my days off, Spanish breads mostly.
Board games are my decompression thing — heavy ones, Twilight Imperium kind of evenings.
Weekday daytimes (I work nights), some Sunday afternoons. Spanish, Catalan, English, basic Dutch.
Linda here. Born in Dordrecht, lived in Rotterdam for fifteen years. Forty, work as a paediatric nurse.
I live in Schiebroek, very quiet, garden out back, perfect for the dog.
Board games are basically my Saturday — I host a group, mostly mid-weight stuff like Wingspan and Brass.
I cook a lot, baking is the part I love most — sourdough every Sunday. Glass of wine while it proves.
Weeknight evenings after seven, Saturday afternoons. Dutch, English, basic German.
I'm Ahmet. Moved from Istanbul nine months ago for an engineering role at the port.
I live in Beverwaard, southern side of the city, cheaper and very quiet.
Cooking is my main joy — Turkish home stuff, slowly learning Dutch produce. I bake börek almost every weekend.
Board games on Friday nights, podcasts on the long commute, wine sometimes.
Weeknight evenings, both weekend days. Turkish, English, basic Dutch.
Hi, I'm Beatrix. Came from Munich last September for a master's in environmental engineering.
I'm in Centrum near Eendrachtsplein, sharing with two other students.
I cook vegan almost daily — mostly out of curiosity. I'm into baking too, especially anything with rye.
Board games are how my flat decompresses, podcasts on the commute. I don't drink much wine but I respect those who do.
Weeknight evenings, both weekend days. German, English, learning Dutch.
I'm Sander, 55, born here. Used to run a small restaurant, now consulting.
I live in Kralingen, near the lake, with my wife and a large dog.
I'd like to help newcomers feel a bit at home — cooking is the easiest way to do that. Big home cook, baker, wine person.
Board games most weekends — I keep getting heavier ones, my friends keep groaning. Podcasts on the commute when I have one.
Weeknight evenings, both weekend days. Dutch, English, Italian, French.
Nia here. Moved from Bucharest seven months ago for a marketing job at a Rotterdam health-tech company.
I'm in Crooswijk, a little flat near the park.
I cook constantly — mostly vegan since I moved here, Romanian comfort food on Sundays. Baking is the other obsession, mostly sourdough.
Board games are my favourite way to meet people. Podcasts on my walk to work.
Weeknight evenings, both weekend days. Romanian, English, beginner Dutch.
I'm Pablo. Came from Buenos Aires a year ago — partner's job. I'm a backend developer.
We're in Delfshaven, old house, two cats, too many books.
Cooking is the family thing — Argentine asados when the weather is even half-decent, baking in winter. Wine I'm slightly snobby about.
Board games most Friday nights. Latin music in the kitchen at all times.
Weeknight evenings, full weekends. Spanish, English, Italian, picking up Dutch.
I'm Floor. Born in Eindhoven, lived in Rotterdam for six years. Twenty-nine, work as a social worker.
I'm in Noord, near Bergweg, with a small balcony I cook on in summer.
Vegan cooking is the daily thing — I'd love to swap recipes with people. Baking on Sundays.
Board games are my Saturday evening default. Podcasts everywhere.
Weeknight evenings, both weekend days. Dutch, English, basic Spanish.
I'm Ines. Moved from Porto last autumn for a senior-nurse role at Erasmus MC.
I'm in Centrum, near the Lijnbaan, walking distance to almost everything.
Cooking is my anchor — Portuguese comfort food, lots of baking, sourdough and broa de milho.
I love wine, mostly Portuguese. Board games on slow Saturdays, podcasts on the way to work.
Weekday daytimes (I work odd shifts), weekend daytimes too. Portuguese, English, beginner Dutch.
Hey, I'm Marco. Came from Milan eight months ago for an architecture firm in Amsterdam.
I'm in Oud-West, near the Kinkerstraat. Loud, cheap-ish dinners, near every venue I care about.
Live music is the reason I picked Amsterdam over Berlin. Jazz mostly, indie sometimes. I draw in venues a lot.
I cycle everywhere, drink local beer, art galleries on slow days.
Weeknight evenings, both weekend days. Italian, English, learning Dutch.
I'm Yusuf. Moved from Cairo for a master's at the Conservatorium last September.
I live in De Pijp, sharing with two other students, cycling to class.
Live music is my degree and my life — jazz and electronic both. I DJ small parties on weekends.
Beer, bars, late conversations. I don't have many other hobbies because I'm always somewhere with sound.
Weeknight evenings, weekend evenings, sometimes Saturday afternoons. Arabic, English, beginner Dutch.
I'm Lotte. Born in Haarlem, lived in Amsterdam for a decade. Thirty-one, graphic designer.
I live in Jordaan, in a canal-side flat above a bar (yes, I hear it).
Live music — jazz especially — is my weekly thing. Cycling everywhere, art galleries, beer in small bars.
I'd love to meet a few new people who hit shows. My circle keeps moving abroad.
Weeknight evenings, Saturday afternoons. Dutch, English, French.
I'm Mariana. Moved from São Paulo four months ago — my partner started a job here.
We're in Oost, near Oosterpark, cycling distance from most venues.
Live music is the heart of it for me — jazz, samba nights, anything in a small room. Beer over cocktails.
Cycling I learned the hard way, now I love it. Art when I can fit it in.
Weeknight evenings, weekend daytimes. Portuguese, English, beginner Dutch.
Pieter, 45. Amsterdam-born, work as a music producer mostly out of a small studio in Noord.
I live in De Baarsjes, in a flat I bought a million years ago.
I want to help newcomers find the real live-music scene — not just the Paradiso headliners.
Jazz is the deepest love, cycling is non-negotiable, beer in small bars is the social setting I default to.
Weeknight evenings, weekend afternoons. Dutch, English, German.
I'm Selma. Came from Paris six months ago for a UX-design role at a fintech.
I live in Westerpark, near the gas works. Cycle through the park every morning.
Live music — jazz especially — is what I do most evenings. Art galleries on Sundays.
Cycling is the way I navigate the city, beer is the social glue.
Weeknight evenings, weekend daytimes, Sunday afternoons. French, English, learning Dutch.
I'm Niko. Moved from Athens two years ago for a sound-engineer job at a recording studio.
I live in Noord, near the IJ. Take the ferry every day, never gets old.
Live music is the job and the obsession. Jazz, anything experimental, the small rooms over the festivals.
Cycling, bars where I know the owner, occasional recording sessions on weekends.
Weeknight evenings, weekend afternoons. Greek, English, intermediate Dutch.
Daria here. Moved from St. Petersburg ten months ago to write for an Amsterdam literary magazine.
I'm in Centrum, near Spui, in a tiny apartment with very tall windows.
Live music is my weekly ritual — jazz on weeknights, more chaotic stuff on weekends. I drink beer like it's research.
Art exhibitions on slow Sundays, walking the canals after a show.
Weeknight evenings, weekend evenings. Russian, English, basic Dutch.
Hey, I'm Henrik. Came from Oslo a year ago for a data-scientist role at a logistics company.
I live in Noord, near the ferry. Run along the IJ every morning.
Running is the constant — I do marathons. Hiking trips when I can. Open-water swimming in the IJ, summer only.
I picked up padel since arriving, surprisingly good fun.
Weekday mornings, weekend mornings, sometimes Sunday afternoons. Norwegian, English, basic Dutch.
I'm Sara. Born in Utrecht, lived in Amsterdam six years. Twenty-eight, physiotherapist.
I live in De Pijp, near Sarphatipark, run the loop before work.
Yoga and running are my baseline — I teach a yoga class on Saturday mornings.
Padel weekly, hiking trips when I can get out of the city.
Weekday mornings, Saturday all day, Sunday mornings. Dutch, English.
I'm Diego. Moved from Medellín a year ago for an engineering role in renewable energy.
I'm in Westerpark, three minutes from the park, run there daily.
Padel is my favourite — I'd play four times a week if I could. Running, hiking, cycling everywhere.
Outdoor weekends are how I recover from desk life.
Weekday mornings before work, both weekend days. Spanish, English, basic Dutch.
I'm Mila. Came from Zagreb last September to do a master's in environmental science at UvA.
I live in Oost, near the Tropenmuseum, sharing with friends.
Yoga is daily, running every other day. Hiking on weekends when we leave the city. Swimming when warm enough.
I want to meet people through sport, that's the easiest way for me.
Weekday mornings, weekend mornings, sometimes Sunday afternoons. Croatian, English, beginner Dutch.
I'm Job. 44, lived in Amsterdam for twenty years, outdoor-pursuits instructor.
I live in Zuid, near the Amsterdamse Bos.
I want to help newcomers find their outdoor people — it's the easiest way to make this city feel like home.
Running, hiking, yoga, padel, all of it. I lead hikes most weekends.
Weekday mornings, weekday evenings, both weekend days. Dutch, English, German.
Aylin here. Moved from Istanbul five months ago for an architecture-firm job.
I'm in Oud-West, near Bilderdijkpark.
Running every morning, yoga twice a week, padel when I can. I just started climbing at a gym in Noord.
Hiking trips on long weekends — I'm trying to see all of the Veluwe.
Weekday mornings, weekend daytimes. Turkish, English, beginner Dutch.
I'm Robin. Came from Antwerp a year and a half ago for a consulting job.
I live in Noord, just past Buiksloterweg.
Running is the main one — half marathons, a marathon every now and then. Padel weekly, hiking long weekends.
I cycle to work, used to commute by car, won't go back.
Weekday mornings, Saturday all day, Sunday morning. Dutch, French, English.
I'm Tess. Born and raised here, thirty-two, primary-school teacher.
I live in De Baarsjes, near the Erasmuspark.
Yoga most mornings, running the rest. Padel club on Wednesdays.
I'd love to find more active people to do weekend hikes with — my work crowd is mostly indoor.
Weekday mornings before school, weekend mornings, Saturday afternoons. Dutch, English, basic German.
Andrei here. Came from Cluj-Napoca eight months ago for an engineering role.
I'm in Westerpark, near the gas works, run there every morning.
Padel is my favourite — I'd play daily if I could. Running, hiking, swimming when the IJ allows.
Outdoor weekends are non-negotiable for me.
